Data

Data report · Florida DBPR public inspection records · June 2026

Emergency orders

An emergency order is the strongest action a Florida inspector can take — reserved for conditions serious enough to stop operations on the spot. 33 restaurants we track have one on record. 17 of them are in Orange County alone.

Where they cluster

  • Orange
    17
  • Volusia
    8
  • Brevard
    5
  • Osceola
    2
  • Seminole
    1

Source: Florida DBPR public inspection records, as of June 2026. Each entry shows the exact emergency disposition and date recorded by the state; the grade shown is the restaurant’s current standing, which may differ from the day of the order. Many of these establishments have since complied and reopened. Methodology →
